coreCompetencies: Basic understanding of training a neural network with PyTorch under supervision
Elementary grasp of reading a research paper and identifying its core method
Able to run an existing training script and log metrics with guidance
Basic understanding of tensors, autograd and the training loop
Able to launch experiments on a GPU following provided instructions
Elementary practice of tracking experiments with tools such as Weights & Biases
Basic understanding of reproducibility through seeds and pinned dependencies
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with Python and NumPy
Familiar with Git and running jobs on a shared cluster
Elementary notion of linear algebra and gradients
indicators: Requires constant supervision
Can reproduce simple experiments with detailed guidance
Needs 3-6 months to become productive

coreCompetencies: Able to reimplement a simple method from a paper and match reported baselines
Grasp of the training loop including loss, optimizer and learning-rate schedule
Able to build data loaders and preprocessing for a training pipeline
Practical debugging of training with loss curves and gradient inspection
Able to run controlled experiments that change one variable at a time
Grasp of mixed-precision training and its effect on memory and speed
Familiar with profiling a training step to find obvious bottlenecks
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with single-GPU memory management and batch sizing
Familiar with experiment tracking and configuration management
Basic practice of unit testing model and data code
indicators: Reproduces published methods with supervision
Resolves medium-complexity issues in training code
Actively participates in code and experiment reviews

coreCompetencies: Proficient at reimplementing methods from recent papers with correct evaluation
Able to set up multi-GPU data-parallel training with tools such as DistributedDataParallel
Able to design ablation studies that isolate the contribution of each component
Solid command of debugging numerical instability such as NaNs and diverging loss
Able to profile and optimize a training step for GPU utilization
Grasp of gradient accumulation and activation checkpointing to fit larger models
Able to build reproducible experiment pipelines with versioned configs and data
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable writing custom PyTorch operators and hooks
Able to read CUDA kernels and reason about memory-access patterns
Basic practice of distributed communication such as all-reduce
indicators: Completes complex research engineering tasks with minimal supervision
Identifies and resolves failures in training and evaluation pipelines
Actively contributes to experimental design alongside scientists

coreCompetencies: Able to build distributed training with sharding such as FSDP or DeepSpeed ZeRO
Proficient with experiment infrastructure that runs and tracks many parallel runs
Able to optimize training throughput through kernel fusion and efficient data loading
Solid command of memory optimization including activation checkpointing and offloading
Able to translate a novel paper method into efficient, tested training code
Proficient with reproducibility practices across hardware, seeds and software versions
Able to write custom CUDA or Triton kernels for a performance-critical operation
complementaryCompetencies: Comfortable with profiling tools such as the PyTorch profiler and Nsight
Able to collaborate with scientists to turn hypotheses into experiments
Grasp of dataset construction and evaluation-harness design
indicators: Leads development of critical research infrastructure
Effectively mentors junior research engineers
Makes informed decisions on training-system design

coreCompetencies: Expert at designing training systems that scale efficiently across many GPUs
Able to architect experimentation platforms that maximize researcher iteration speed
Proficient at performance optimization across the compute, memory and communication stack
Able to combine tensor, pipeline and data parallelism for large-model training
Solid command of profiling and eliminating bottlenecks in distributed training
Able to build evaluation harnesses that produce trustworthy, reproducible results
Expert at writing and optimizing custom kernels for novel model operations
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of numerical methods and low-precision training stability
Able to design benchmarks that measure training efficiency
Grasp of the research process from hypothesis to publication
indicators: Designs training and experimentation systems for a research group
Defines technical standards for reproducible research engineering
Leads cross-team initiatives on research infrastructure

coreCompetencies: Able to architect research computing platforms shared across many research teams
Expert at scaling training infrastructure to hundreds of GPUs reliably
Proficient with the tooling that turns research ideas into reproducible experiments at scale
Able to design systems resilient to hardware failure during long training runs
Solid command of performance engineering across kernels, parallelism and interconnect
Able to lead adoption of new training frameworks and hardware across the org
Expert at reliability engineering for long-running distributed training jobs
complementaryCompetencies: Aware of emerging accelerators and their programming models
Able to design shared evaluation and benchmarking infrastructure
Grasp of the economics of research-compute allocation
indicators: Architects research infrastructure for multiple teams
Influences organizational decisions on research computing
Represents the company technically in research engineering venues

coreCompetencies: Provides technical leadership for research engineering across an organization
Able to design research-compute and training architectures for large-scale programs
Expert at strategies for maximizing research throughput per unit of compute
Solid command of hardware-agnostic training stacks across accelerator vendors
Able to define multi-year roadmaps for training and experimentation infrastructure
Deep grasp of the cost and efficiency trade-offs of large training programs
Expert at building and scaling research engineering teams
complementaryCompetencies: Able to evaluate emerging training methods and hardware for adoption
Able to co-author publications with research scientists
Aware of trends in efficient training and systems for machine learning
indicators: Defines organizational strategy for research engineering
Recognized as a technical authority in training systems
Speaks at systems and machine-learning conferences

coreCompetencies: Sets the strategic vision for the organization's training and experimentation infrastructure
Able to lead major shifts in how research compute is built and used
Expert at technical due diligence for research infrastructure and hardware investment
Drives organizational change toward efficient, reproducible research at scale
Able to build and lead research engineering organizations
Deep grasp of the impact of infrastructure decisions on research velocity
Expert at stakeholder relations between research, engineering and leadership
complementaryCompetencies: Able to influence industry practice on training systems
Able to guide investment in compute and research tooling
Aware of the accelerator and research-infrastructure landscape
indicators: Impacts the technical direction of research engineering in the field
Advises leadership on research-infrastructure strategy
Mentors senior research engineers

coreCompetencies: Provides executive leadership for research engineering across the organization
Able to align research-infrastructure strategy with scientific and business goals
Expert at managing large budgets for research compute and hardware
Commands stakeholder and board communication on research-infrastructure investment
Able to lead the transformation of how the organization runs research at scale
Deep grasp of risk management for large, long-running training programs
Expert at building competitive advantage through research engineering capability
complementaryCompetencies: Able to shape organizational policy on compute allocation and research practice
Able to build partnerships with hardware and cloud providers
Aware of the economics of large-scale research programs
indicators: Defines and executes the research engineering technology vision
Accountable for research-compute strategy and budget
Serves as the public voice of the company in research engineering
